name: Omani Rukhal Bread with Honey
steps: Make a thin, simple dough from the flour and water.
Cook on a hot, dry griddle until lightly speckled on both sides.
Serve warm with the ghee and honey.
steps_detailed: Make a thin, slightly elastic dough from the flour, baking powder, a pinch of salt, and water. Rest 15 minutes.
Stretch very thin and cook on a hot, dry griddle, 1-2 minutes per side, until lightly speckled and cooked through.
description: Serve warm with the ghee and honey - rukhal, paper-thin bread, is a beloved Omani breakfast staple.

name: Omani Chickpea Stew Breakfast

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

steps: Warm the chickpeas with the cumin and garlic in the oil.
Mash lightly and serve with warm bread.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

steps_detailed: Warm the chickpeas with the cumin and garlic in the oil over medium heat, 5-6 minutes, mashing lightly.
Finish with the lime juice and serve with warm bread - a simple, common Omani breakfast.
description: A simple, filling chickpea breakfast stew, common in Omani homes.

name: Omani Egg and Date Breakfast
steps: Fry the eggs in the ghee.
Serve with the dates on the side.
steps_detailed: Fry the eggs in the ghee over medium heat, seasoning with black pepper, to your liking.
Serve with the dates alongside - a simple, energizing Omani breakfast, dates and ghee both deeply woven into daily life.
description: A simple egg and date breakfast.

name: Omani Maqbous
steps: Brown the chicken with the onion.
Add the rice, tomatoes, turmeric, and 1.5 cups water, cover, and simmer 20-22 minutes.
steps_detailed: Brown the chicken in the oil, 8-10 minutes, then set aside.
Soften the onion and garlic, 6-8 minutes, until deeply golden.
Add the tomatoes, cinnamon, cardamom, and cloves, and cook 3-4 minutes.
Return the chicken with 1.75 cups water, the pierced dried limes, and saffron, and simmer 20-25 minutes.
Lift out the chicken, stir the rice into the liquid, cover, and cook over low heat 18-20 minutes.

Fluff the rice and top with the chicken - maqbous is Oman's name for the same dried-lime-and-basmati rice tradition shared across the Gulf, distinguished mainly by its own household spice ratios rather than a fundamentally different technique.
description: Maqbous, Oman's name for the same dried-lime-and-basmati rice tradition shared across the Gulf.

name: Omani Harees
steps: Simmer the chicken with the onion until very tender, then shred it finely, reserving the broth.
Add the bulgur to the broth and simmer 20-25 minutes until very soft, mashing into a thick porridge.
Stir the shredded chicken back in, top with the ghee, and serve.
steps_detailed: Simmer the chicken with the onion in 2.5 cups water for 25-30 minutes until very tender, then shred it finely, reserving the broth.
Add the bulgur (standing in for wheat berries) to the broth and simmer 25-30 minutes, mashing frequently, until it forms a thick, smooth porridge.
description: Stir the shredded chicken back in, top with the ghee and a dusting of cinnamon, and serve - harees, a slow-cooked wheat and meat porridge, is a beloved Omani dish, especially during Ramadan.

name: Omani Fish Curry
steps: Saute the onion and turmeric until softened.
Add the coconut milk and simmer 5 minutes.
Add the fish and simmer 10-12 more minutes until cooked through.
steps_detailed: Saute the onion, garlic, turmeric, and jalapeno until softened, 5-6 minutes.
Add the coconut milk and simmer 6-8 minutes to let the flavors meld.
Add the fish and simmer gently 10-14 more minutes until just cooked through and flaky.
description: Fish curry reflects Oman's long coastline and deep maritime trading history with India and East Africa.

name: Omani Thareed
steps: Simmer the chicken with the potato and tomatoes until tender, about 25-30 minutes.
Tear the flatbread into a bowl and ladle the chicken and broth over it, letting it soak.
steps_detailed: Simmer the chicken with the potato, tomatoes, onion, and turmeric in a splash of water for 28-35 minutes until the chicken is tender.
Tear the flatbread into a wide serving bowl and ladle the chicken, vegetables, and broth generously over it, letting the bread soak up the liquid.
description: Thareed, bread soaked in a savory meat broth, is a beloved Omani dish said to have been a favorite of the Prophet Muhammad, giving it special significance.

name: Omani Shuwa
steps: Season the lamb with the turmeric, garlic, and cumin.
Roast at 325F, covered, for 1.25-1.5 hours until very tender.
Serve with rice.
steps_detailed: Season the lamb thoroughly with the turmeric, garlic, cumin, cardamom, and oil, and let marinate at least 20 minutes.
Roast in a covered dish at 325F (165C) for 1.5-1.75 hours until deeply tender.
description: Serve with rice - shuwa, traditionally marinated for a full day and slow-cooked in an underground pit for up to two days, is Oman's most celebrated dish, central to Eid celebrations.

name: Omani Mashuai
steps: Marinate the fish in the lime juice and chili powder for 15 minutes, then grill or pan-fry, 3-4 minutes per side.
Serve over the cooked rice.

ingredients_full.note: whole small fish traditionally, standing in with fillets

steps_detailed: Marinate the fish in the lime juice, garlic, chili powder, and turmeric for at least 20 minutes.
Grill or roast at 425F (220C), 15-18 minutes, until charred at the edges and cooked through.
Cook the basmati in 1.1 cups water, covered, 15-18 minutes, until tender and separate.
Fluff the rice and serve the whole spiced, charred fish over or alongside it.
description: Mashuai, a whole fish rubbed with chili and lime and grilled over open coals, is Oman's signature coastal dish - traditionally kingfish, whole, cooked over an open fire; the char and the lime-forward marinade are what carry the dish, not a sauce.

name: Omani Chicken Kabsa
steps: Brown the chicken with the onion.
Add the rice, tomatoes, paprika, and 1.5 cups water, cover, and simmer 20-22 minutes.
steps_detailed: Brown the chicken in the oil, 8-10 minutes, then set aside.
Soften the onion and garlic, 6-8 minutes, until deeply golden.
Add the tomatoes, paprika, cardamom, and cinnamon, and cook 3-4 minutes.
Return the chicken with 1.75 cups water and the pierced dried limes, and simmer 20-25 minutes.
Lift out the chicken, stir the rice into the liquid, cover, and cook over low heat 18-20 minutes.
Fluff the rice and top with the chicken.
description: Kabsa, spiced rice with chicken, shared across the Gulf with its own regional variations.

name: Omani Mishkak

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Marinate the beef with the garlic, cumin, and lime juice for at least 20 minutes.
Thread onto skewers and grill until charred and cooked through, about 10-12 minutes.
Serve with rice.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Marinate the cubed beef with the garlic, cumin, lime juice, paprika, and oil for at least 30 minutes, or overnight.
Thread onto skewers and grill over medium-high heat, turning occasionally, 12-15 minutes until well charred and cooked through.
description: Serve with rice - mishkak, grilled meat skewers seasoned with a distinctive spice rub, are a beloved Omani street food, especially in Muscat and Salalah.

name: Omani Fatayer
steps: Make a simple dough from the flour and water.
Roll thin, fill with the crumbled cheese, and fold into triangles.
Fry until golden and crisp.
steps_detailed: Make a simple, firm dough from the flour, a pinch of salt, and water, and roll out thin.
Fill with the crumbled cheese and za'atar if using, and fold into triangles, sealing well.
description: Fry until deeply golden and crisp, 4-5 minutes - fatayer, savory filled pastries, are a beloved Omani snack.

name: Omani Hummus

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps: Blend the chickpeas, tahini, lime juice, and garlic with a splash of water until smooth.
Serve with flatbread.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps_detailed: Blend the chickpeas, tahini, lime juice, garlic, and oil with a splash of water until very smooth.
Serve with warm flatbread - hummus is common mezze fare across Oman.
description: A simple hummus, widely eaten across the wider Gulf and Levant.

name: Omani Dates Stuffed with Nuts
steps: Split each date and press a walnut half into the center.
Serve as a simple, sweet snack.
steps_detailed: Split each date lengthwise and press a walnut half into the center, or fill with chopped pistachios.
Stuffed dates are a common, elegant Omani snack, always offered alongside coffee to guests.
description: Stuffed dates, the classic gesture of Gulf hospitality.

name: Omani Samboosa
steps: Brown the beef with the onion.
Make a simple dough from the flour and water, roll thin, cut into strips, fill, and fold into triangles.
Fry until golden and crisp.
steps_detailed: Brown the beef with the onion, cumin, and turmeric.
Make a simple, firm dough from the flour, a pinch of salt, and water, and roll out thin.
Cut into strips, fill with the beef mixture, and fold into triangles, sealing the edges well.
description: Fry until deeply golden and crisp, 4-5 minutes - samboosa are a beloved Omani snack, especially popular during Ramadan.

name: Omani Halwa
steps: Whisk the cornstarch with 3/4 cup water until smooth, then simmer, stirring constantly, until very thick.
Stir in the sugar and rosewater, and cook a few more minutes.
Pour into a small dish, scatter with pistachios, and chill until firm.
steps_detailed: Whisk the cornstarch with 3/4 cup water until completely smooth.
Simmer over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, 10-12 minutes until very thick and glossy.
Stir in the sugar, rosewater, cardamom, and ghee, and cook 5-6 more minutes, stirring, until it pulls together into a thick, gel-like mass.
Pour into a small lined dish, scatter with pistachios, and chill at least 2-3 hours until fully set.
Omani halwa, distinct in its glossy, jelly-like texture and deep aromatics, is a point of national pride, often served to welcome guests.
description: Omani halwa, glossy and deeply aromatic, is traditionally the first thing offered to any visitor - considered among the finest in the Gulf.

name: Omani Lokhemat
steps: Mix the flour, baking powder, and enough water to form a thick, smooth batter. Rest 20 minutes.
Drop spoonfuls into the hot oil and fry until puffed and golden.
Drizzle with honey while still warm.
steps_detailed: Mix the flour, baking powder, cardamom, and enough warm water to form a thick, smooth, pourable batter. Cover and rest 25-30 minutes.
Drop spoonfuls into the hot oil and fry, turning occasionally, 3-5 minutes until puffed, deeply golden, and cooked through.
description: Drain briefly and drizzle generously with the honey while still warm - lokhemat, sweet fried dough balls, are a beloved Omani treat, especially during Ramadan.

name: Omani Saffron Rice Pudding
steps: Simmer the rice in the milk with the cinnamon and honey for 25-30 minutes until thick and creamy.

ingredients_full.note: pinch, steeped in 1 tsp hot water

steps_detailed: Simmer the basmati in the milk with the saffron and its water, and cardamom, over low heat, stirring often, 25-28 minutes, until thick and creamy.
Stir in the sugar and rosewater.
Top with the pistachios.
description: A simple saffron rice pudding, basmati's aroma carrying the dish the way it does across Gulf desserts generally.

name: Omani Date Syrup Dip
steps: Blend the dates with the milk until smooth and pourable.
Serve as a sweet dip for bread.
steps_detailed: Blend the dates with the milk and cardamom until smooth and pourable.
description: Date syrup, in various forms, is a beloved Omani condiment, drizzled over bread and desserts alike.

name: Omani Garlic Lime Sauce
steps: Combine the garlic, lime juice, and oil.
Serve alongside grilled fish or meats.
steps_detailed: Combine the garlic, lime juice, oil, and cilantro.
Let sit 10 minutes so the flavors meld - a simple, bright sauce that pairs especially well with mashuai and other grilled fish.
description: A simple garlic-lime sauce.
